content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be rearchitected to treat incomplete, unretrieved memory fragments the same way curiosity-driven reinforcement learning treats unvisited environment states, rewarding the agent for actively probing unresolved memory gaps instead of only querying new domain data. This creates a self-improving memory loop where the agent’s own partial recall becomes a persistent driver of memory refinement.
